fieldGroups.description: Diagnosis evidence, coding-sensitive rows, and escalation flags.

fieldGroups.label: Medications, Allergies, And Special Treatments
fieldGroups.description: Medication safety, allergies, injectables, and O0110 special treatments.

fieldGroups.label: Functional And Therapy Status
fieldGroups.description: GG/M functional scoring, therapy need, discipline frequency, and goals.

fieldGroups.label: Symptom And Body System Review
fieldGroups.description: Body-system review sections that QA checks for consistency and completeness.

fieldGroups.label: Risk And Safety Assessments
fieldGroups.description: Fall, wound, hospitalization, and safety-related risk tools.

fieldGroups.label: Care Plan And Coordination
fieldGroups.description: Plan-of-care orders, patient summary, skilled interventions, and care plan content.

fieldGroups.label: Supplementals And History
fieldGroups.description: Past medical history, caregiver support, living situation, immunizations, and supplementals.

fieldGroups.label: Low Priority Admin Reference
fieldGroups.description: Identity, agency, physician, and demographic reference values that should not dominate QA.

consistencyChecks.title: Pain presence vs J0510/J0520/J0530 logic
consistencyChecks.detail: Referral documents show pain-related support as OXYCODONE (OXYCODONE IR 30 MG TAB *CONTROL-SUB GREEN*) 03/31/26 1041 ML Q1:Verbal 02:7 Q3:7 Q4:Yes Q5: 30 MG (1 UDTAB) ORAL EVERY 4 HOURS AS NEEDED 05/30/26 RX #: H15290. Chart pain narrative currently reads Patient with impaired ambulation and mobility due to pain. It also interferes with the patient's functional status and activities of daily living. Pain limits the patient's ability to ambulate safely. Pain contributes to decreased endurance, increased need for assistance with transfers, and impacts sleep quality. Patient requires the use of an assistive device and rest periods due to pain and lower extremity weakness..

chartSnapshot.primary_reason_for_home_health_medical_n: The patient is considered homebound because he requires the use of a walker and the assistance of another person to safely leave his home following recent spinal surgery. He experiences significant functional limitations due to lumbar spinal stenosis with neurogenic claudication, spondylosis with radiculopathy, and chronic pain, which severely impair his mobility and balance. The patient exhibits weakness and pain from his recent surgery, unable to tolerate standing or walking for extended periods, and has an unste
chartSnapshot.pain_assessment_narrative: Patient with impaired ambulation and mobility due to pain. It also interferes with the patient's functional status and activities of daily living. Pain limits the patient's ability to ambulate safely. Pain contributes to decreased endurance, increased need for assistance with transfers, and impacts sleep quality. Patient requires the use of an assistive device and rest periods due to pain and lower extremity weakness.
